title: landolphia tomentosa
intro: Landolphia tomentosa, also known as the African berry or West African wild olive, is a species of flowering plant in the dogbane family Apocynaceae. It has been traditionally used in West African folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits associated with this plant include the treatment of various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its potential to reduce blood pressure and improve cardiovascular health. Landolphia tomentosa can be prepared into herbal remedies, including teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often used to harness its medicinal properties.
